nodes.x: My son, keep my words, and lay up my commandments with thee.
My son, if thou wilt receive my words, and hide my commandments with thee;
My son, give ear to my words; for I swear unto you, that inasmuch as ye shall keep the commandments of God ye shall prosper in the land.
Now therefore, my son, obey my voice according to that which I command thee.

My son, give ear to my words, for I say unto you, even as I said unto Helaman, that inasmuch as ye shall keep the commandments of God ye shall prosper in the land; and inasmuch as ye will not keep the commandments of God ye shall be cut off from his presence.
Thou therefore, my son, be strong in the grace that is in Christ Jesus.
Keep my commandments, and live; and my law as the apple of thine eye.
¶ If ye walk in my statutes, and keep my commandments, and do them;
My son, forget not my law; but let thine heart keep my commandments:
If they break my statutes, and keep not my commandments;
not keep my commandments, they shall have no part or lot in the household of faith.
Therefore shall ye keep my commandments, and do them: I am the Lord.
Bind them upon thy fingers, write them upon the table of thine heart.
Bind them continually upon thine heart, and tie them about thy neck.
Bow down thine ear, and hear the words of the wise, and apply thine heart unto my knowledge.
If thou prepare thine heart, and stretch out thine hands toward him;
Let not mercy and truth forsake thee: bind them about thy neck; write them upon the table of thine heart:
Let them not depart from thine eyes; keep them in the midst of thine heart.
Say unto wisdom, Thou art my sister; and call understanding thy kinswoman:
Thou shalt not uncover the nakedness of thy mother’s sister: for she is thy mother’s near kinswoman.
Thou shalt not uncover the nakedness of thy father’s sister: she is thy father’s near kinswoman.

I can say to the sisters, if you have superior talents, arise and let
your light shine. Prove to your neighbors and the community that you
are capable of teaching those sisters whom you deem to be ignorant or
neglectful. 7:162.
And again, verily I say unto you, to some is given, by the Spirit of God, the word of wisdom.
That they may keep thee from the strange woman, from the stranger which flattereth with her words.
To keep thee from the evil woman, from the flattery of the tongue of a strange woman.
To deliver thee from the strange woman, even from the stranger which flattereth with her words;
That it may be well with thee, and thou mayest live long on the earth.
That they may fear thee, to walk in thy ways, so long as they live in the land which thou gavest unto our fathers.
And she said, According unto your words, so be it. And she sent them away, and they departed: and she bound the scarlet line in the window.
¶ For at the window of my house I looked through my casement,
¶ And he brought me to the door of the court; and when I looked, behold a hole in the wall.
And verily I say unto you, let this house be built unto my name, that I may reveal mine ordinances therein unto my people;
To that above the door, even unto the inner house, and without, and by all the wall round about within and without, by measure.
And beheld among the simple ones, I discerned among the youths, a young man void of understanding,
The young man saith unto him, All these things have I kept from my youth up: what lack I yet?
To give subtilty to the simple, to the young man knowledge and discretion.
Passing through the street near her corner; and he went the way to her house,
She came where Jesus stood beside the way. lie look'd upon her, and his heart was moved.
And it came to pass, when she saw that he had left his garment in her hand, and was fled forth,
He pursued them, and passed safely; even by the way that he had not gone with his feet.

Then, noiselessly, she got out of bed, stepped into slippers, and drew a
robe about her. Just as silently she left her room and walked down the
hall to Mrs. DeBrugh's bedroom.

Soon after this, by her own request, she was assisted to cross the
street to her father-in-law's, who was at that time prostrated upon a
bed of sickness. When she entered the house he cried out in amazement,
"Lovisa is dead, and her spirit is now come to warn me of my sudden
departure from this world." "No, father," she exclaimed, "God has
raised me up, and I have come to tell you to prepare for…
In the twilight, in the evening, in the black and dark night:
They meet with darkness in the daytime, and grope in the noonday as in the night.
Let the stars of the twilight thereof be dark; let it look for light, but have none; neither let it see the dawning of the day:

“The Morning Breaks; the Shadows Flee” By Elder Bruce R. McConkie Of the Council of the Twelve “The morning breaks; the shadows flee; Lo, Zion’s standard is unfurled! … The dawning of a brighter day Majestic rises on the world.” ( Hymns, no. 269.) When the sun goes down and the dusk of day deepens into night, then darkness reigns. During the night, darkness is everywhere and the vision of all is…
And, behold, there met him a woman with the attire of an harlot, and subtil of heart.
And it came to pass that he placed his heart upon his riches, and he spent his time in riotous living with his wives and his concubines; and so did also his priests spend their time with harlots.
¶ Then came there two women, that were harlots, unto the king, and stood before him.
Then he asked the men of that place, saying, Where is the harlot, that was openly by the way side? And they said, There was no harlot in this place.
And he began to be lifted up in the pride of his heart, and to wear very costly apparel, yea, and even began to establish a church after the manner of his preaching.
And being found in fashion as a man, he humbled himself, and became obedient unto death, even the death of the cross.
(She is loud and stubborn; her feet abide not in her house:
Remove thy way far from her, and come not nigh the door of her house:

[_She pulls her children to the ground, and throws herself along with
them in his way._
She looketh well to the ways of her household, and eateth not the bread of idleness.
Her feet go down to death; her steps take hold on hell.
Now is she without, now in the streets, and lieth in wait at every corner.)
¶ Wisdom crieth without; she uttereth her voice in the streets:
Passing through the street near her corner; and he went the way to her house,

This is the rejoicing city that dwelt carelessly, that said in her heart, I am, and there is none beside me: how is she become a desolation, a place for beasts to lie down in! every one that passeth by her shall hiss, and wag his hand.
So she caught him, and kissed him, and with an impudent face said unto him,

As she said this she looked up into the face of her lover, and drawing
him down to her, kissed him on the lips.
And she caught him by his garment, saying, Lie with me: and he left his garment in her hand, and fled, and got him out.
With her much fair speech she caused him to yield, with the flattering of her lips she forced him.
And when she had brought them unto him to eat, he took hold of her, and said unto her, Come lie with me, my sister.
As soon as she heard that, she arose quickly, and came unto him.
I have peace offerings with me; this day have I payed my vows.
I will pay my vows unto the Lord now in the presence of all his people,
I will pay my vows unto the Lord now in the presence of all his people.
I will go into thy house with burnt offerings: I will pay thee my vows,
¶ And if ye offer a sacrifice of peace offerings unto the Lord, ye shall offer it at your own will.
And arise up and be more careful henceforth in observing your vows, which you have made and do make, and you shall be blessed with exceeding great blessings.
Therefore came I forth to meet thee, diligently to seek thy face, and I have found thee.
When thou saidst, Seek ye my face; my heart said unto thee, Thy face, Lord, will I seek.
But if from thence thou shalt seek the Lord thy God, thou shalt find him, if thou seek him with all thy heart and with all thy soul.
And he said, My presence shall go with thee, and I will give thee rest.
And if the Lord shall say—Be thou accursed, that no man shall find thee from this time henceforth and forever—behold, no man getteth it henceforth and forever.
I will answer thee, and thy companions with thee.
I have decked my bed with coverings of tapestry, with carved works, with fine linen of Egypt.
Fine linen with broidered work from Egypt was that which thou spreadest forth to be thy sail; blue and purple from the isles of Elishah was that which covered thee.
I decked thee also with ornaments, and I put bracelets upon thy hands, and a chain on thy neck.
I have perfumed my bed with myrrh, aloes, and cinnamon.
¶ While the king sitteth at his table, my spikenard sendeth forth the smell thereof.
And satest upon a stately bed, and a table prepared before it, whereupon thou hast set mine incense and mine oil.
Spikenard and saffron; calamus and cinnamon, with all trees of frankincense; myrrh and aloes, with all the chief spices:
And cinnamon, and odours, and ointments, and frankincense, and wine, and oil, and fine flour, and wheat, and beasts, and sheep, and horses, and chariots, and slaves, and souls of men.
And oil for the light, and spices for anointing oil, and for the sweet incense,
Come, let us take our fill of love until the morning: let us solace ourselves with loves.
Beloved, if God so loved us, we ought also to love one another.

The Power of God’s Love By Elder John H. Groberg Of the Presidency of the Seventy Filled with His love, we can endure pain, quell fear, forgive freely, avoid contention, renew strength, and bless and help others. What is it about true love that touches every heart? Why does the simple phrase “I love you” evoke such universal joy? Men give various reasons, but the real reason is that every person…
For the goodman is not at home, he is gone a long journey:
But know this, that if the goodman of the house had known in what watch the thief would come, he would have watched, and would not have suffered his house to be broken up.
And this know, that if the goodman of the house had known what hour the thief would come, he would have watched, and not have suffered his house to be broken through.
And wheresoever he shall go in, say ye to the goodman of the house, The Master saith, Where is the guestchamber, where I shall eat the passover with my disciples?
And David said, Is there yet any that is left of the house of Saul, that I may shew him kindness for Jonathan’s sake?
He hath taken a bag of money with him, and will come home at the day appointed.
In that day, he which shall be upon the housetop, and his stuff in the house, let him not come down to take it away: and he that is in the field, let him likewise not return back.
And on the morrow when he departed, he took out two pence, and gave them to the host, and said unto him, Take care of him; and whatsoever thou spendest more, when I come again, I will repay thee.
If there be laid on him a sum of money, then he shall give for the ransom of his life whatsoever is laid upon him.
Otherwise he shall receive the money which he has paid, and shall leave the place, and shall be cut off out of my church, saith the Lord God of hosts;
Then he is gracious unto him, and saith, Deliver him from going down to the pit: I have found a ransom.
With her much fair speech she caused him to yield, with the flattering of her lips she forced him.
So she caught him, and kissed him, and with an impudent face said unto him,
Whereupon he promised with an oath to give her whatsoever she would ask.
And he laid his hands on her: and immediately she was made straight, and glorified God.
He goeth after her straightway, as an ox goeth to the slaughter, or as a fool to the correction of the stocks;
He that goeth about as a talebearer revealeth secrets: therefore meddle not with him that flattereth with his lips.
And truly the Son of man goeth, as it was determined: but woe unto that man by whom he is betrayed!
He that troubleth his own house shall inherit the wind: and the fool shall be servant to the wise of heart.
He that goeth forth and weepeth, bearing precious seed, shall doubtless come again with rejoicing, bringing his sheaves with him.
¶ Shall horses run upon the rock? will one plow there with oxen? for ye have turned judgment into gall, and the fruit of righteousness into hemlock:
Till a dart strike through his liver; as a bird hasteth to the snare, and knoweth not that it is for his life.
For he is cast into a net by his own feet, and he walketh upon a snare.
Lest thou learn his ways, and get a snare to thy soul.
And shall begin to smite his fellowservants, and to eat and drink with the drunken;
The snare is laid for him in the ground, and a trap for him in the way.
And he shall sprinkle upon him that is to be cleansed from the leprosy seven times, and shall pronounce him clean, and shall let the living bird loose into the open field.
¶ Hearken unto me now therefore, O ye children, and attend to the words of my mouth.
Now therefore hearken unto me, O ye children: for blessed are they that keep my ways.
Hear me now therefore, O ye children, and depart not from the words of my mouth.
O hearken, ye elders of my church, and give ear to the words which I shall speak unto you.
Verily, I say unto you, that woe shall come unto the inhabitants of the earth if they will not hearken unto my words;

1. O hearken, ye Elders of my Church, and give ear to the words
    which I shall speak unto you;
Let not thine heart decline to her ways, go not astray in her paths.
Our heart is not turned back, neither have our steps declined from thy way;
¶ Keep thy heart with all diligence; for out of it are the issues of life.
For she hath cast down many wounded: yea, many strong men have been slain by her.

Now many women and children had been slain with the sword, and also many of their flocks and their herds; and also many of their fields of grain were destroyed, for they were trodden down by the hosts of men.

They have set her a bed in the midst of the slain with all her multitude: her graves are round about him: all of them uncircumcised, slain by the sword: though their terror was caused in the land of the living, yet have they borne their shame with them that go down to the pit: he is put in the midst of them that be slain.
They shall fall in the midst of them that are slain by the sword: she is delivered to the sword: draw her and all her multitudes.
And in her was found the blood of prophets, and of saints, and of all that were slain upon the earth.

He saw that there had been slain by the sword already nearly two millions of his people, and he began to sorrow in his heart; yea, there had been slain two millions of mighty men, and also their wives and their children.
Her house is the way to hell, going down to the chambers of death.
For her house inclineth unto death, and her paths unto the dead.
But he knoweth not that the dead are there; and that her guests are in the depths of hell.
